title: Chaetanthera dioica
taxonomy: Chaetanthera dioica is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae, a group of flowering plants that includes many species of daisies, everlasting flowers, and other similar plants. Within the tribe, Chaetanthera dioica is placed in the genus Chaetanthera, which comprises several species of flowering plants native to South America. The exact classification of Chaetanthera dioica is as follows: family Asteraceae, tribe Gnaphalieae, genus Chaetanthera, species C. dioica. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and genetic characteristics.

intro: Chaetanthera dioica, also known as Argentine yerba de mayo, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The health benefits of Chaetanthera dioica include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
